explanation: All Section 2 criteria are satisfied. 2.1: Confirmed cervical radiculopathy at C5-C6 with nerve root compression and dermatomal radicular symptoms. 2.3: MRI within 6 months interpreted by a radiologist demonstrates structural pathology consistent with symptoms and surgical level. 2.2(a): 8 weeks of supervised PT with documented functional outcomes directed at the cervical condition exceeds the 6-week minimum. 2.2(b): Two qualifying medications (naproxen NSAID for 6 weeks and gabapentin for 5 weeks) each exceed the 4-week minimum. 2.2(c): One cervical epidural steroid injection at C5-C6 satisfies the interventional requirement. 2.4: Physician provided attestation, clinical rationale, functional limitations, and statement of surgical risks and alternatives. Therefore authorization is approved.
